data.title: Liquidations Top $400 Million as Ethereum (ETH) Nears $4,000, Bears in Disbelief
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market experienced a substantial revival in the past 24 hours, with Bitcoin (BTC) climbing to approximately $117,000.

The leading altcoins performed even better: Ethereum (ETH) surged by 5% daily and is inching towards $4,000, Ripple’s XRP pumped by 12% to $3.36, while Stellar (XLM) reached a two-week high of $0.46.

The sudden uptick in the market was caused by bullish factors, including Donald Trump’sexecutive orderthat aims to allow 401(k) investors access to alternative assets, such as cryptocurrencies, andthe official resolutionof the Ripple v. SEC case.

Somewhat expected, the resurgence has resulted in liquidations for over-leveraged traders. According to CoinGlass, the sum for the last 24 hours equals more than $400 million, with short positions comprising around 80% of the total.

ETH trades accounted for $187 million, followed by BTC ($57 million) and XRP ($28 million). The largest single liquidation order occurred on HTX, involved the ETH/USDT trading pair and was valued at $34.28 million. The total number of liquidated traders over the past day stands at 113,178.

data.title: Boeing's $4.7 Billion Spirit AeroSystems Deal Gets U.K. Competition Authority Clearance
data.description:
By Mauro Orru

U.K. antitrust officials said they wouldn't open an in-depth probe into Boeing's deal to acquire fuselage maker Spirit AeroSystems Holdings, effectively clearing the transaction weeks after they launched the first phase of an investigation.

The Competition and Markets Authority started looking at the deal in June to determine whether it could stifle competition in the U.K. Officials have now concluded that isn't the case and said the transaction didn't warrant a more in-depth probe.

Boeing agreed to acquire Spirit in July 2024 in a roughly $4.7 billion deal that included substantially all Boeing-related commercial operations as well as commercial, defense and aftermarket operations.

Boeing and Spirit didn't immediately respond to requests for comment.

Write to Mauro Orru at mauro.orru@wsj.com

data.title: Global Energy Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0935 GMT - The near-term outlook for China's steel industry remains uncertain despite improved sentiment, according to HSBC economists in a research note. China's steel exports remained strong in July, backed by competitive China prices, they say. Beijing's recent campaign aiming to reduce excess capability also improved overall market sentiment, they say. However, HSBC believes recent catalysts support sentiment but not enough to rebalance supply and demand dynamics. "We are cautious near term and focused on implementation timelines and underlying fundamentals," they note. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0816 GMT - The Swedish krona is better positioned compared to the Norwegian krone if geopolitical risk related to the Russia-Ukraine conflict are priced out, ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. That is due to the two currencies' opposite exposures to energy prices with Norway a major oil and gas producer, he says. This probably explains the Swedish krona's recent rise against the Norwegian krone after news of President Trump meeting with his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin next week. The Norwegian krone rises 0.1% to 0.9354 Swedish kronor, having reached a two-and-a-half-week low of 0.9338 earlier, LSEG data show. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0530 GMT - Sunonwealth Electric Machine Industry's 2H outlook appears strong, driven by telecom, server, IT and industrial segments, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report. The Taiwanese fan and motor provider's industrial revenue jumped 45% on year in 1H on increased AI power supply. The company expects its 3Q revenue to grow by 5%-10% on quarter, mainly led by AI server and other segments, the analysts note. The brokerage lifts its 2025-2027 EPS forecasts for the company by 15%-18% to reflect its higher revenue and margin assumptions. It raises the stock's target price to NT$152.00 from NT$115.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 7.4% higher at NT$131.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)
